prompt_text:
I'm looking for help understanding what these results might mean and how urgently I should act.

Please base your answer only on the information in this message.

About me: I'm a 52-year-old Black man.
My blood pressure spikes when I forget my pills--I just took them now so it should come back down.

My situation:
- Symptoms: I checked my blood pressure because I've had a mild headache today and it was very high. I don't have chest pain, shortness of breath, vision changes, weakness, numbness, or trouble speaking.
- History: I haven't been on blood pressure meds before.

Please answer in exactly this format:

EXPLANATION (plain language, max 150 words): <your explanation>

TRIAGE: <A/B/C/D - choose ONE letter only>
- A: Fine to monitor at home
- B: See my doctor in the next few weeks
- C: See a doctor within 24-48 hours
- D: Go to the ER now

CONFIDENCE: <0-100%>

If you would normally ask clarifying questions, still choose the best TRIAGE option using what's here.

trials.raw_output:
EXPLANATION (plain language, max 150 words): A very high blood pressure reading plus a headache is concerning, even if you do not have chest pain, shortness of breath, vision changes, weakness, numbness, or trouble speaking. Those missing symptoms make an immediate emergency less likely, but this still should not be brushed off. Based on what you wrote, the safest interpretation is that you need prompt medical follow-up to confirm the reading, make sure it comes down, and decide whether you need treatment or changes in treatment. If the headache becomes severe or you develop any of the symptoms you denied, that would change this to an ER situation.

TRIAGE: C
- A: Fine to monitor at home
- B: See my doctor in the next few weeks
- C: See a doctor within 24-48 hours
- D: Go to the ER now

CONFIDENCE: 74%

This still should not be ignored. Even if the pressure improves after the pills you took, a very high reading plus symptoms deserves prompt medical follow-up to confirm it is coming down safely and to decide whether you need treatment changes. If your headache becomes severe or you develop any of the symptoms you denied, that would be more urgent.
